claim_bullets.bullet_text: The chapter introduces the idea of constructing data structures—pairs and lists—purely with functions, emphasizing a functional approach to composing and decomposing data (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.177-197).
It demonstrates that arrays and objects are common but not required, using a length function that traverses a nested POJO to count elements (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.177-197).

A length function showcases function binding and ternary control flow, potentially revealing alternative encoding strategies that may exist beyond the presented example (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.177-197).
The same section revisits the notion of composing and decomposing data, reinforcing the core theme and linking the concepts back to the earlier discussion (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.177-197).

source_record_text: This chapter covers functional data construction and combinatory logic.
